issues_to_create.title: Project Setup and Core Architecture
issues_to_create.description:
Set up the Python project structure, configuration management, and database layer.

## Requirements
- Create Python package structure (mcp_healthcare/)
- Implement Pydantic config manager for MCP_DATA_DIR, MCP_DB_NAME, MCP_LOG_LEVEL, MCP_AUDIT_ENABLE
- Initialize SQLite database with WAL mode
- Create audit_log table (ts INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id TEXT, query_hash TEXT, response_summary TEXT)
- Create Click CLI entrypoint with --help
- Create Pydantic data models: MedicationInput, ObservationBundle, InteractionAlert, PathwayStep

## Test Steps
1. Run 'python -m pytest tests/test_config.py' - verifies config loading from env vars
2. Run 'python -m pytest tests/test_db.py' - verifies database initialization and audit table creation
3. Run 'mcp-healthcare --help' - prints usage info

issues_to_create.title: Drug Interaction Checker
issues_to_create.description:
Implement the drug interaction checking tool that evaluates medication lists against a curated interaction database.

## Requirements
- Accept JSON array of medication objects ({name, dose, route})
- Reference interaction tables from MCP_DATA_DIR/interactions.sqlite
- Output JSON list of interaction alerts with severity, description, source
- Write audit record for every query (timestamp, user-id, query-hash, result-count)
- CLI command: mcp-healthcare drug-check

## Test Steps
1. python -m pytest tests/test_drug_interaction.py::test_empty_list → expects []
2. python -m pytest tests/test_drug_interaction.py::test_known_interaction → expects one alert with severity 'contraindication'
3. python -m pytest tests/test_drug_interaction.py::test_audit_write → verifies new row in audit_log table

issues_to_create.title: Diagnostic Criteria Evaluator
issues_to_create.description:
Implement the diagnostic criteria evaluation tool that checks clinical observations against rule sets.

## Requirements
- Receive JSON observation bundle ({vitals, labs, history})
- Load rule definitions from MCP_DATA_DIR/rulesets/<name>.json
- Evaluate all required criteria; return {passed: bool, evidence: [string]}
- Append audit entry for each evaluation
- CLI command: mcp-healthcare diagnose

## Test Steps
1. python -m pytest tests/test_diagnostic.py::test_met_criteria → expects passed: true
2. python -m pytest tests/test_diagnostic.py::test_missing_labs → expects passed: false with evidence listing missing lactate
3. python -m pytest tests/test_diagnostic.py::test_audit → confirms audit row contains rule set name

issues_to_create.title: Care Pathway Recommender
issues_to_create.description:
Implement the care pathway recommendation tool that suggests ordered care actions.

## Requirements
- Accept {diagnosis: string, context: object}
- Pick highest-priority pathway from MCP_DATA_DIR/pathways/<diagnosis>.json
- Output JSON array of ordered steps with action, detail, estimated_hours
- Log recommendation event to audit table
- CLI command: mcp-healthcare recommend

## Test Steps
1. python -m pytest tests/test_pathway.py::test_known_diagnosis → returns non-empty ordered list
2. python -m pytest tests/test_pathway.py::test_unsupported_diagnosis → returns empty list and logs warning
3. python -m pytest tests/test_pathway.py::test_audit_integrity → checks audit entry includes recommendation count

issues_to_create.title: Integration Testing and CLI Polish
issues_to_create.description:
End-to-end integration tests and CLI polish for the complete MCP-Healthcare server.

## Requirements
- Integration test running all three CDS tools in sequence
- Verify audit log completeness across all operations
- Verify CLI help prints within 200ms
- Verify no external network imports (requests, urllib, httpx, etc.)
- Verify no more than 5 third-party pip packages
- Polish CLI output formatting

## Test Steps
1. python -m pytest tests/test_integration.py::test_full_workflow → runs drug-check, diagnose, recommend in sequence
2. python -m pytest tests/test_integration.py::test_audit_completeness → all operations logged
3. python -m pytest tests/test_integration.py::test_no_network_imports → verifies offline operability
4. python -m pytest tests/test_integration.py::test_startup_latency → CLI help under 200ms

meta_issue.title: [META] Project Progress Tracker
meta_issue.description:
Session tracking issue for MCP-Healthcare CDS Server project.

This issue tracks progress across coding sessions. Each session adds a comment with:
- What was accomplished
- Current status of all features
- What to work on next
- Any blockers or notes
